etymology.description: Depicts a tall building or tower
etymology.interpretation: The character 京 (jīng, "tower") is a pictogram representing a tall building or a tower. Its form directly illustrates the concept of height and grandeur associated with a prominent structure.

examples.chinese: 故宫的塔楼很高(故宮的塔樓很高)。
examples.english: The tower of the Forbidden City is very tall.

examples.chinese: 在北京(在北京)，你可以看到许多高楼大厦(你可以看到許多高樓大廈)。
examples.pinyin: Zài Běijīng, nǐ kěyǐ kàn dào xǔduō gāolóu dàshà.
examples.english: In Beijing, you can see many tall buildings.

examples.chinese: 古代的建筑中常常能见到高耸的塔顶(古代的建築中常常能見到高聳的塔頂)。
examples.pinyin: Gǔdài de jiànzhú zhōng chángcháng néng jiàn dào gāosǒng de tǎdǐng.
examples.english: In ancient architecture, towering spires can often be seen.
